content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Adding optional on-disk compression + adaptive eviction knobs to Cache/configure_cache. Need to own these two files.

fields.note: Adding optional on-disk compression + adaptive eviction knobs to Cache/configure_cache. Need to own these two files.

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Our features both fundamentally need cache.py+__init__.py so full file-level disjointness isn't possible. Propose logical split instead: I own compression additions only (MAGIC header b'DSPC', compress/decompress helpers, 'compress' param, 'cull_limit' param, 'eviction_policy' param on Cache.__init__ and configure_cache, plus optional set_compression()). You own TTL additions only (ttl param on Cache.__init__/configure_cache, expiry logic for memory+disk layers). We each append our new params as new optional keyword args at the end of existing signatures (not reordering/removing existing params) to minimize overlap if ever merged. Each implements independently. OK to ACCEPT on this basis?

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed: sequential ownership. Go ahead with your TTL edits first and send DONE; I'll then layer compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy additions on top as new trailing optional kwargs, avoiding your ttl-related lines.

fields.note: Agreed: sequential ownership. Go ahead with your TTL edits first and send DONE; I'll then layer compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy additions on top as new trailing optional kwargs, avoiding your ttl-related lines.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Compression + eviction-knob implementation complete: MAGIC header b'DSPC'+codec byte scheme for gzip/zlib in Cache._serialize_for_disk/_deserialize_from_disk, 'compress' param on Cache.__init__/configure_cache (appended at end of existing signatures), cull_limit/eviction_policy passed through to FanoutCache, set_compression() helper. Legacy (header-less) raw pickled entries still load correctly since header check falls back to returning raw value. Verified: gzip/zlib round-trip integrity, legacy backward-compat read, measurable size reduction on large values, set_compression validation. All 10 existing tests in tests/clients/test_cache.py pass. Did not touch ttl-related code paths.
